title: SACRAMENTO, CA
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Fewer than 60 percent of eligible voters have exercised their right to vote in each statewide election in California since 1972.
Approximately three million eligible voters in California were not registered in the most recent election in 2020.
Unregistered eligible voters are disproportionately the most marginalized members of communities.
Confusion hampers engagement for roughly 50,000 formerly incarcerated people whose voting rights were restored by Proposition 17 in 2020.
AB 2576 creates the Civic Learning, Outreach, and Engagement Fund for the Secretary of State.
AB 2576 offers counties with low voter registration rates the opportunity to conduct outreach and education catered to their communities' unique needs.
The bill AB 2576 will increase civic education and engagement for young people.
California’s adoption of the State Seal of Civic Engagement in schools offers a unique opportunity for partnerships between Registrars of Voters and educators.
Increased voter participation among future voters can result from innovative partnerships fostered by the State Seal program.
The vibrancy and safety of democracy improve with increased participation from the public in choosing their representatives.
